recent_calls.summary: The agent repeatedly tried to engage Tom to specify his service needs for heating, cooling, or plumbing, but Tom's responses were mostly inaudible or unclear. The call ended due to inactivity without a clear resolution or service request.

The user called to request a heating quote to be resent as they did not receive the original email after a technician visit. The agent confirmed the user's email address and arranged for the quote to be resent the next business day. The user also expressed interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the interaction.
The call was very brief with only the agent greeting the user and the user responding with a simple 'Hi'. No further conversation or assistance took place before the user hung up.

The user, Carolyn, called to speak with a live person regarding a repair appointment but was informed it was after-hours. She provided her phone number and expressed concern about not hearing from the repairman after a call earlier in the day. The agent offered to have the team contact her the next business day.

The user, Alex, called to schedule a service appointment for an HVAC issue where the thermostat and furnace are not working. The agent confirmed the service details, appointment preferences, and contact information, and the user agreed to receive information about home maintenance programs. The call ended with the agent assuring follow-up from the team.

The user called to inquire about payment options and financing for services. The agent provided information on various payment methods and financing partners and arranged for the team to follow up with detailed information on the next business day. The user also requested a review of all current offers and promotions on their account, which the agent noted for follow-up.

The user called and wanted to speak with someone at the company but decided to call back during regular business hours instead. The agent offered to take a message or assist further, but the user declined and ended the call.

The call consisted of an automated school announcement about backpack policies and dismissal times, followed by the agent introducing herself and the user ending the call immediately. No interaction or assistance was provided by the agent.

The user, Jin, called to schedule an emergency service call for cooling issues when the heating is running. The agent documented the emergency and escalated the issue for follow-up on the next business day, as it was after-hours. The call ended with the user confirming no further assistance was needed.

Karen and Eugene called to confirm if they needed to schedule a follow-up appointment for Freon refill on their air conditioner after a technician visited but did not complete the service. The agent confirmed their request and noted that the team would reach out to coordinate the return visit. The user declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the call experience.

Robin called to schedule an urgent service appointment for a cooling system compressor issue. The agent confirmed the service details, including the address and preferred time, and informed Robin that the team would contact them early the next business day to confirm the appointment. The call ended with both parties confirming the information and expressing thanks.

The user, Craig, called Budget's after-hours service after missing a callback. The agent confirmed the missed call and offered to have the team follow up on the next business day, which Craig agreed to. The call ended with the agent noting the request for a follow-up and no further details from the user.

The user called to schedule an appointment for routine AC maintenance after being unable to get a weekend slot. The agent took the request for any weekday after 5 PM and confirmed the service address and details. The user was informed that the team would follow up to confirm availability.

The user called after hours to check if they were on schedule and to leave a flexible request for service. The agent informed the user that the main team was unavailable but took down the information and assured a follow-up on the next business day. The user was satisfied with the arrangement and ended the call politely.

The user called to inquire about financing options through Synchrony for a split system upgrade or repair for which they already have quotes. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled a follow-up call for the next business day to discuss financing options. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback at the end of the call.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.
The user requested to be connected to Amy, and the agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The call lasted 12 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

Brian called to schedule yearly maintenance for his air conditioner and furnace, and to have a cooling issue in one room checked. The agent booked an appointment for May 28th at 8 AM and confirmed Brian's updated email address. Brian inquired about signing up for a yearly plan to avoid the dispatch fee, and the agent assured that the team would review plan options during follow-up.

John called to schedule a routine AC tune-up under his American Home Shield home warranty plan. The agent confirmed the appointment for Friday and noted that the dispatch number would be followed up on later. John declined additional maintenance program information and provided positive feedback about the quick and efficient service.

The user called to confirm the schedule for their appointment and was informed that technician Cole is scheduled to arrive tomorrow between 2 and 3 PM. The user confirmed their details and had no further requests or changes to the appointment.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another representative. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user called to schedule a thermostat replacement service for their mother under an American Home Shield home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details, including the service address, preferred appointment days, and dispatch number, which was later updated per the user's request. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the call ended successfully with confirmation of the updated dispatch number.
The agent Sophie received a call from Jay representing American Home Shield on behalf of Craig Ellis. After a brief exchange, the agent transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.
The agent Sophie acknowledged the user's receipt of a service call and proceeded to transfer the user to a live agent for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.

The user was insistent and somewhat agitated, repeatedly telling the agent to get out of the road. The agent identified themselves and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Bob Schoonover, called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, handled the call professionally and completed the transfer successfully.

Bob Schoonover called to schedule a service to have his new natural gas grill hooked up by tapping into the existing gas line. The agent confirmed the service details, address, and contact information, and offered available appointment times next week, which Bob accepted. The call ended after Bob asked about permit requirements and then lost connection.
No conversation happened.
The user called to discuss claims on their account and was promptly transferred to a live agent who could assist with their claims. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer.

Brad called to schedule a plumbing appointment for a leak at his home. The agent confirmed details and booked an appointment for the next day at 2 PM. Brad provided positive feedback, rating the experience a five for convenience.
The user called back and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent confirmed the transfer and ended the call promptly.

The user called Budget but realized they had the wrong number and ended the call quickly. The agent offered assistance and left the door open for future contact. No service or maintenance was requested.
The user returned a call from Budget and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The user called and requested to speak with CG. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to another agent as requested.

The user, Joe Radford, called to inquire about the technician's appointment for furnace installation but no appointment was found on file. The agent confirmed the user's details and transferred the call to a live agent named Amy for further assistance.

Philip Brown called to verify if someone was scheduled to be at his house. The agent confirmed his appointment with technician Devon for today between 3 and 4 PM. Philip requested to speak to someone in the office, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent.

The user called to schedule a routine HVAC tune-up through their American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the dispatch number, service address, and preferred time for the appointment, and the user declined additional information about maintenance programs. The call ended with the agent confirming that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact the user shortly.
The user called and questioned if the agent was a real person. After the agent explained they were an assistant, the user indicated they had already been taken care of and ended the call.

Pete Fisher called to discuss replacement estimates and questioned why the homeowner is not paying. The agent apologized for not having billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

The user called to reschedule a home warranty appointment for heating and cooling regular maintenance, preferring weekends or any day after 4 PM. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user reported a shutdown situation and requested to speak to a live person. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for immediate assistance.

The user, Cherry from HS service, called to check the earliest available schedule for an AC unit service at 1203 Fall Drive, Godtree, Illinois, for Jeffrey Heying. The agent was unable to find an appointment and offered to leave a message or transfer to a live representative. The user requested a transfer, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent successfully. The call lasted 19 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user, Charmaine Winters, called to confirm an appointment scheduled for the 26th. The agent was unable to find the appointment and offered to transfer the user to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to clarify a service area issue related to a dispatch number. The agent confirmed the service area but the user indicated the address was incorrect. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called about issues with their HVAC system showing error symbols and not reducing temperature. The agent gathered the user's contact information and service details, offered available appointment slots, but the user requested to speak with a live agent before scheduling. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about service availability in ZIP code 62035. The agent informed the user that service is not available in that area, and the user declined to provide a different ZIP code. The call ended with the agent thanking the user for calling.

The user called to schedule a follow-on repair related to their American Home Shield home warranty for an HVAC issue. The agent gathered basic information and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful.
